name: V.S. Naipaul

acts.description: Physically beat his long-term mistress Margaret Murray; documented in Patrick French's authorized biography
Contemptuous dismissal of women writers as sentimentalists; deep-seated public misogyny

behavior_summary: Nobel laureate who physically abused his mistress over years, detailed in the authorized biography. Also publicly stated women writers were inferior, sentimental, and unworthy of serious consideration.

art.summary: Nobel Prize-winning prose stylist. Among the greatest English-language writers of the 20th century by most serious measures.

verdict.blurb: A Nobel laureate who beat his mistress and believed women couldn't write. His prose is magnificent. His biography reads like a case study in why we sanctify literature's monsters.
